questions.question: Akham Lakshmi Devi is associated with which of the following dance form?
questions.explanation: Akham Lakshmi Devi is a renowned classical dancer known for her contributions to the Manipuri dance tradition. She received the SangeetNatakAkademi Award in 2018 for her work in Manipuri dance.

questions.question: Match the following: List I (Alloy) List II (Components) A) German Silver 1) Copper + Zinc B) Gunmetal 2) Copper + Nickel + Zinc C) Brass 3) Copper + Tin D) Bronze 4) Copper + Tin + Zinc
questions.explanation: The correct matching is : A-2, B-4, C-1, D-3 Examples of common alloys: 1. Brass – Copper + Zinc 2. Bronze – Copper + Tin 3. Steel – Iron + Carbon 4. Stainless Steel – Iron + Chromium + Nickel 5. Solder – Lead + Tin 6. Duralumin – Aluminium + Copper + Magnesium + Manganese 7. Nichrome – Nickel + Chromium + Iron 8. German Silver (Nickel Silver) – Copper + Nickel + Zinc 9. Magnalium – Aluminium + Magnesium 10. Gunmetal – Copper + Tin + Zinc 11. Invar – Iron + Nickel 12. Alnico – Aluminium + Nickel + Cobalt + Iron

questions.question: Guntapalle Chaitya Buddhist cave is situated in which of the following states?
questions.explanation: The Guntupalli Buddhist Caves (Guntapalle Chaitya) are ancient Buddhist rock-cut caves located in Eluru district of Andhra Pradesh and date back to around the 2nd century BCE. They were constructed mainly during the rule of the Satavahana Dynasty. The Satavahanas were major patrons of Buddhism in the Deccan region and supported the construction of many Buddhist monuments.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a Statutory Body in the context of Indian polity?
questions.explanation: In Indian Polity, government bodies are mainly classified into three types based on how they are created. 1. Constitutional Bodies- These bodies are directly created by the Constitution of India. Their powers, functions, and structure are mentioned in the Constitution itself. Examples: • Election Commission of India • Union Public Service Commission • Finance Commission • Comptroller and Auditor General of India • National Commission for Scheduled Castes 2. Statutory Bodies - These bodies are created by an Act of Parliament or State Legislature. Their powers and functions are defined in the law that creates them. Examples: • National Human Rights Commission • Central Information Commission • Central Vigilance Commission • National Green Tribunal • Lokpal 3. Non-Constitutional / Executive Bodies- These bodies are created by executive orders or government resolutions. They are not mentioned in the Constitution or created by law. Examples: • NITI Aayog • National Development Council

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Pradhan Mantri Dhan‑Dhanya Krishi Yojana: 1. The scheme aims to benefit about 1.7 crore farmers across the country. 2. The scheme was approved on 16 July 2025 with duration of six years. 3. The scheme will be implemented in 200 districts across India and covers at least two districts from every State and Union Territory. Which of the following statement is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Pradhan Mantri Dhan-Dhanya Krishi Yojanawas approved by the Union Cabinet on 16 July 2025 and is planned for a duration of six years (2025–26 to 2030–31). The scheme aims to benefit about 1.7 crore farmers and will be implemented in 100 districts across India, with at least one district selected from each State and Union Territory. Therefore, statement 3 is incorrect.

questions.question: The ruler of which of the following dynasties invaded Malaya to free its trade with China?
questions.explanation: Chola dynesties invaded malaya to free its trade with China. The most important victory of the Chola dynasty ruler Rajendra I (1014-1044 AD) was the successful military campaign against the Sri Vijaya Empire, which extended to the Malaya Peninsula, Sumatra, Java and adjacent islands. The objective of this campaign was to increase the trade activities of the Cholas with the eastern world (China)

questions.question: Who has been awarded the Pritzker Architectue Prize 2025?
questions.explanation: The Pritzker Architecture Prize 2025 was awarded to Chinese architect Liu Jiakun. He is known for architecture that focuses on community life, local materials, and cultural context

questions.question: The breaking up of the rocks on the earth’s surface is called ______________ .
questions.explanation: The process in which rocks break down into smaller pieces at or near the Earth’s surface due to physical, chemical, or biological factors is called Weathering. other options: • Conurbation – It refers to the merging of several cities into a large urban area. • Desertification – It is the process by which fertile land becomes desert. • Flacking – This is not a recognized geological term for rock breakdown.

questions.question: Which organisation developed the SAKSHAM counter-Unmanned Aircraft System grid launched by the Indian Army in 2025?
options: Defence Research and Development Organisation Hyderabad
questions.explanation: The SAKSHAM (Situational Awareness for Kinetic Soft and Hard Kill Assets Management) counter-UAS grid was developed in collaboration with Bharat Electronics Limited. It is an indigenous command-and-control system that helps detect, track, and neutralize hostile drones for the Indian Army

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true about Volleyball game? 1. Volleyball was introduced in Asian games in 1951 for men. 2. Volleyball was introduced in Summer Olympics in 1964. 3. The standard size of a Volleyball court is 18 m x 9 m.
questions.explanation: Only 2 and 3 are correct statements. Volleyball was first introduced in the Asian Games in 1958 (Tokyo), not in 1951. Volleyball became an Olympic sport at the 1964 Tokyo Summer Olympics. The standard volleyball court size is 18 m long and 9 m wide Other important facts : Volleyball was invented in 1895 by William G. Morgan in the United States. The international governing body of volleyball is Fédération Internationale de Volleyball (FIVB), established in 1947. Each team has 6 players on the court at a time. Net height • Men: 2.43 m • Women: 2.24 m In Volleyball, a special defensive player is called the Libero

questions.question: Which of the following is not an example of a transverse wave?
questions.explanation: In transverse waves, particles vibrate perpendicular to the direction of wave motion. Light waves, water waves, and waves on a string are transverse. Sound waves are longitudinal waves.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the chairman of the Indian Banks’ Association (IBA) in March 2025?
questions.explanation: In March 2025, Challa Sreenivasulu Setty, the Chairman of State Bank of India, was elected as the Chairman of the Indian Banks’ Association. He succeeded MV Rao and will serve until the next Annual General Meeting of the association.

questions.question: Arrange the following Himalayan peaks from West to East. A) Nanga Parbat B) Mount Everest C) Kanchenjunga D) Nanda Devi
questions.explanation: From west to east in the Himalayas: A → D → B → C Nanga Parbat (Pakistan, westernmost among these) Nanda Devi (Uttarakhand, India) Mount Everest (Nepal–China border) Kanchenjunga (India–Nepal border, eastern side)

questions.question: Padma Shri Awardee Gosaveedu Shaik Hassan is known for playing which of the following instruments?
questions.explanation: Gosaveedu Shaik Hassan was a renowned classical musician from Andhra Pradesh and an expert player of the traditional South Indian wind instrument Nadaswaram. He performed for many decades in temples, especially at the Bhadrachalam temple, and was posthumously awarded the Padma Shri in 2022 for his contribution to music.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true about Gautamiputra Satakarni? • He reissued the coins of Shaka ruler Nahapana with his own insignia. • The Nashik inscription describes him as Lord of Vindhya • Gatha Sattasai was similar to Sangam poetry and is attributed to Gautamiputra Satakarni.
questions.explanation: Statement 1 – Correct After defeating the Western Kshatrapa ruler Nahapana, Gautamiputra Satakarni overstruck (reissued) Nahapana’s silver coins with his own symbols. Statement 2 – Correct The Nashik inscription of his mother Gautami Balashri praises him and refers to his extensive rule including the Vindhya region. Statement 3 – Incorrect The work Gatha Sattasai (Gaha Sattasai) is attributed to the Satavahana king Hala, not to Gautamiputra Satakarni.

questions.question: On which of the following component of chloroplast is chlorophyll arranged?
questions.explanation: In the chloroplast, chlorophyll pigments are located in the membranes of the Thylakoid. These thylakoid membranes are arranged in stacks called grana and are the sites where the light reactions of Photosynthesis occur. The Stroma is the fluid surrounding the thylakoids where the dark reactions (Calvin cycle) take place.

questions.question: In March 2025, NITI Aayog released 2nd North Eastern Region District SDG Index. Which of the following is not correct?
options: The index was released by NITI Aayog in collaboration with the Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region and UNDP.
Hnahthial district of Mizoram has the highest scoring district in the entire Northeast Region.
Longding district of Manipur has the Lowest scoring district in the entire Northeast Region.
The Index evaluates progress toward 15 SDGs and covers 121 districts of North East Region.
questions.explanation: Option 3 is incorrect. Longding is in Arunachal Pradesh, not Manipur. Key facts about the 2nd North Eastern Region District SDG Index (2023–24) released by NITI Aayog: • Developed by NITI Aayog in collaboration with the Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region with technical support from the United Nations Development Programme. • It covers 121 districts of the Northeast. • It tracks progress on 15 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). • Highest scoring district: Hnahthial (Mizoram) – 81.43. • Lowest scoring district: Longding (Arunachal Pradesh).

questions.question: Which type of muscles do the uterus, iris of the eye, and bronchi contain?
questions.explanation: The uterus, iris of the eye, and bronchi are organs that function involuntarily, meaning their movement is not controlled consciously. These organs contain Smooth muscle. Characteristics of smooth muscles: • They work automatically (involuntary). • They are non-striated (no stripes under microscope). • Found in walls of internal organs. Examples: • Uterus → contracts during childbirth • Iris of the eye → controls pupil size • Bronchi → regulate airflow in lungs

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Santiniketan is correct? 1. Santiniketan is a cultural and educational town in Birbhum district, West Bengal, founded by Rabindranath Tagore in the early 20th century. 2. Santiniketan was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2025
questions.explanation: Only statement 1 is correct. Santiniketan was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2023, not 2025 “Santiniketan” means “Abode of Peace”. Santiniketan was founded by Rabindranath Tagore in 1901 as an experimental school that promoted open-air education and learning close to nature. It later became Visva-Bharati University in 1921, established by Rabindranath Tagore to promote global cultural exchange. Important cultural festivals celebrated here include Poush Mela and Basant Utsav

questions.question: According to Census 2011, what is the estimated percentage of people below the poverty line in urban areas?
questions.explanation: Based on poverty estimates used alongside the 2011 census period and calculated by the Planning Commission of India using the Tendulkar methodology, about 25.7% of the rural population, 13.7% in urban population and21.9% overall in India was estimated to be below the poverty line.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution mentions that ‘No person shall be prosecuted and punished for the same offence more than once’?
questions.explanation: Article 20 of the Constitution of India provides three important protections to individuals in criminal law. 1. Article 20(1) of the Constitution of India Protection against ex-post-facto laws. No person can be punished for an act which was not an offence at the time it was committed. Also, a heavier punishment cannot be imposed than what was prescribed at the time of the offence. 2. Article 20(2) of the Constitution of India Protection against double jeopardy. No person shall be prosecuted and punished for the same offence more than once. 3. Article 20(3) of the Constitution of India Protection against self-incrimination. No accused person can be compelled to be a witness against himself (cannot be forced to confess or give evidence against himself).

questions.question: Which part of the bifocal lens facilitates near vision?
questions.explanation: A bifocal lens has two parts: • Upper part – used for distant vision and usually contains a concave lens (for myopia). • Lower part – used for near vision and contains a convex lens (for reading or hypermetropia). When a person looks down to read, the lower segment of the lens helps focus nearby objects. Hence the correct answer is D. Lower part with convex lens

questions.question: Which of the following is correct regarding gross fiscal deficit?
options: Gross fiscal deficit = Net borrowing at home – Borrowing from RBI – Borrowing from abroad
Gross fiscal deficit = Net borrowing at home – Borrowing from RBI + Borrowing from abroad
Gross fiscal deficit = Net borrowing at home + Borrowing from RBI + Borrowing from abroad
Gross fiscal deficit = Net borrowing at home + Borrowing from RBI – Borrowing from abroad
questions.explanation: Gross Fiscal Deficit represents the total borrowing requirement of the government. It includes borrowings from domestic sources, the central bank, and foreign sources. Thus, the relationship is: Gross Fiscal Deficit = Net borrowing at home + Borrowing from RBI + Borrowing from abroad

questions.question: What is the weight of the shot put for men's and women's category respectively?
questions.explanation: The weight of the shot put used in athletics is: • Men: 7.26 kg (16 pounds) • Women: 4 kg (8.82 pounds)

questions.question: In context of British rule and Indian National Movement, which of the following statement is correct? I. On 12 August 1765, the Mughal emperor appointed the East India Company as the Diwan of Bengal. II. In 1801, a subsidiary alliance was imposed on Awadh, and in 1856 it was taken over. III. Bahadur Shah Zafar died in the Rangoon jail in November 1862.
questions.explanation: All statements I, II and III are correct After the Battle of Buxar, Shah Alam II granted the Diwani (revenue collection rights) of Bengal, Bihar, and Orissa to the East India Company on 12 August 1765. Awadh accepted the subsidiary alliance in 1801 under Lord Wellesley. Later, it was annexed in 1856 by Lord Dalhousie on the ground of misgovernance. Bahadur Shah Zafar, the last Mughal emperor and symbolic leader of the Indian Rebellion of 1857, was exiled to Rangoon and died there in November 1862.

questions.question: Two runners, Vishal and Ravi, start from the same point on a circular track of 500m. They run in the same direction with speeds of 10 m/s and 8 m/s, respectively. How many times will they cross each other in 25 minutes?
questions.explanation: Since both are running in the same direction, Relative speed = 10 − 8 = 2 m/s They will meet each time Vishal gains one full lap (500 m) over Ravi. Time to meet once = Track length / Relative speed = 500/2 = 250 seconds Total time = 25 minutes = 25 × 60 = 1500 seconds Number of crossings = Total time / Time per meeting = 1500/250 = 6 They will cross each other 6 times.

questions.question: If (a+b+c)= 25 and (a2+b2+c2)=400, find the value of (a-b)2+(b-c)2+(c-a)2.
questions.explanation: Using identities : (a - b)² + (b - c)² + (c - a)² = 2(a² + b² + c² - ab - bc - ca) &(a + b + c)² = a² + b² + c² + 2(ab + bc + ca) 25² = 400 + 2(ab + bc + ca) 625 = 400 + 2(ab + bc + ca) 2(ab + bc + ca) = 225 ab + bc + ca = 112.5 Now substitute into the first identity: (a - b)² + (b - c)² + (c - a)² = 2(400 - 112.5) = 2 × 287.5 = 575

So Possible value of n = 6 For divisible by 9, sum of digits will be divisible by 9. i.e. (31+m+n) or (37+m) will be divisible by 9. So possible value of m = 8 Required ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774247137Math%20Paid%20-%2012_html_c0d51a36.png)

questions.explanation: For similar solids: Volume ratio = (ratio of corresponding lengths)³ ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774247137Math%20Paid%20-%2012_html_2671d572.png)

questions.question: P and Q take 18 and 8 days more than (P+Q) respectively to do a piece of work. If P works for first 10 days then in how many days will Q alone do the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Let P and Q can together do the whole work in x days. Then, P alone can do in (x+18) days and Q alone can do in (x+8) days. So, x= √(8×18)=√144=12 P alone can do in 30 days Q alone can do in 20 days. Work done by P in 10 days = 10/30=1/3 Remaining, work i.e (1 - 1/3 = 2/3) completed by Q = 20×2/3=13 (1/3) days

questions.question: Sum of A and B is 150% more than the sum of C and D. C is 25 % more than D. If A is 100% more than B and D is 400, then find the value of A.
questions.explanation: Given that, (A+B) = (C+D)×250% = (A+B)/(C+D)=5/2 ………..[1] And, C = D×125% = C/D=5/4 = C/400 = 5/4 i.e. C = 500 Put values of C and D in equation [1] (A+B)/900 = 5/2 i.e. A+B = 2250 Also given that, A=B×200% i.e. A : B = 2 : 1 So, A = 2/3 × 2250=1500

questions.question: If the coordinate of the vertices are A(2,3), B(7,3) and C(3,6), what is Centroid of the tringle?
questions.explanation: Formula for centroid of a triangle: Centroid = {(x1+x2+x3)/3, (y₁ + y₂ + y₃)/3} Substitute the points: {(2+7+3)/3, (3+3+6)/3}=(4,4)

questions.question: A bag contains Rs 10, Rs 5, and Rs 2 coins in the ratio of 1:2:3. If the total amount of money in the bag is Rs 1404. Find the number of coins of Rs 5.
questions.explanation: Amount of coins = Rs. 10, Rs. 5, Rs. 2 Ratio of coins = 1 : 2 : 3 Let numbers = a, 2a, 3a Total amount = 10×a+5×2a+2×3a=1404 = 26a = 1404 i.e. a=54 Number of 5 Rs. Coins = 2a = 108

questions.question: A shopkeeper marks his goods 25% above the cost price. He allows a discount of 10% on the marked price. It is found that the selling price of 15 items is equal to the cost price of 20 items. Find the percentage profit or loss.
Two candidates ran in an election; 10% abstained from voting. 300 votes were declared invalid, and the winner received 60% of the vote count and won by 900 votes. Find the number of valid votes.
questions.explanation: Let total number of voters = T 10% abstained, so votes cast = 90% of T = 0.9T Invalid votes = 300 So, valid votes = 0.9T − 300 Now, winner got 60% of total voters = 0.6T Since there are only two candidates, the loser got the remaining valid votes: Loser’s votes = Valid votes − Winner’s votes = (0.9T - 300) - 0.6T = 0.3T - 300 Now, as per question, Winner − Loser = 900 0.6T - (0.3T - 300) = 900 0.3T + 300 = 900 0.3T = 600 T = 2000 Valid votes = 0.9T - 300= 1800-300 = 1500

questions.question: Find the lateral surface area of the square pyramid whose base perimeter is 40 cm, and slant height is 10 cm.
questions.explanation: Lateral Surface Area = 1/2 × Perimeter of base × Slant height =1/2 × 40 × 10 = 20 × 10 = 200 cm²

questions.question: In a trapezium, the parallel sides are in the ratio 3 : 5. The distance between them is 14 cm. If the area of the trapezium is 448 cm², find the length of the median of the trapezium.
questions.explanation: Let the parallel sides be 3x and 5x. Height h = 14 cm Area = 448 cm² Formula for area of trapezium: Area = 1/2 × (sum of parallel sides) × height 448 = 1/2 × (3x + 5x) × 14 448 = 1/2 × 8x × 14 448 = 4x × 14 x = 8 So, the parallel sides are: 3x = 24 cm and 5x = 40 cm Now, length of median: Median = 1/2 × (sum of parallel sides) =1/2 × (24 + 40) =1/2 × 64 = 32 cm

questions.question: A student takes 1.25 hour to travel form home to school at a speed of 4km/h. By what percentage should he increase his speed to reduce the time by 20% to cover the same distance from school to home?
questions.explanation: For the same distance, speed is inversely proportional to time. If time decreases by 20%, speed increases by: Required % increase in speed = 20 / (100 - 20) × 100 = 20 / 80 × 100 = 1 / 4 × 100 = 25%

questions.question: A chord of length 21 cm is drawn in a circle having diameter 29 cm. What is minimum distance of other parallel chord of length 20 cm in the same circle from 21 cm long chord?
questions.explanation: Distance of 21 cm chord from centre = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774247137Math%20Paid%20-%2012_html_2b6a538c.png) Distance of 20 cm chord from centre = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774247137Math%20Paid%20-%2012_html_7693e897.png) For minimum distance → both on same side of centre Distance = |10.5 - 10| = 0.5 cm

questions.question: A committee of 3 men and 4 women is to be formed from a group of 7 men and 8 women. In how many ways can the committee be formed?
questions.explanation: Number of ways to choose 3 men from 7 = ⁷C₃ = 35 Number of ways to choose 4 women from 8 = ⁸C₄ = 70 Total number of committees = 35 × 70 = 2450

questions.question: 9 girls and one boy went to a hotel for lunch. If each girl spent ₹40 and the boy spent ₹7200 more than the average, how much did the boy spend in total?
questions.explanation: The boy spent ₹7200 more than the average. That extra ₹7200 must be balanced by the deficiency of the 9 girls. So, distribute ₹7200 among 9 girls: 7200 ÷ 9 = 800 This means each girl is ₹800 below the average. Each girl actually spent ₹40. So, Average spending = 40 + 800 = ₹840 Now, boy spent ₹7200 more than average: Boy’s spending = 840 + 7200 = ₹8040

questions.question: A shopkeeper mixes two varieties of tea costing ₹180 per kg and ₹250 per kg. In what ratio should they be mixed so that by selling the mixture at ₹240 per kg, he makes a profit of 20%?
questions.explanation: Selling price = 240 and Profit = 20 % Cost price = 240/120 × 100=200 So, using allegation method, 180 250 200 50 20 5 : 2

questions.question: A person borrows ₹50,000 at 10% per annum compound interest. He pays Rs. 20,000 at the end of the first year and Rs. 25,000 at the end of the second year. How much amount he should pay for the end of the third year so that the entire loan will be paid?
questions.explanation: Amount after 1 year = 50000 × 1.10 = 55000 Payment made = 20000 Remaining amount = 55000 − 20000 = 35000 Amount after 2nd year = 35000 × 1.10 = 38500 Payment made = 25000 Remaining amount = 38500 − 25000 = 13500 Amount after 3rd year = 13500 × 1.10 = 14850 He should pay ₹14,850 at the end of the third year to complete the entire loan.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
options: Despite being the most senior member of the
board, Mr. Khanna is not enough experienced
to handle a crisis of such immense proportions.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 👉 Replace "enough experienced" with "experienced enough". 📌 Error concept: The error lies in the incorrect placement of the adverb "enough" in relation to the adjective "experienced." Grammatical concept: The word "enough" has a very specific "Examiner-favorite" rule regarding its position in a sentence: 1.With Adjectives/Adverbs: When "enough" modifies an adjective or an adverb, it MUST be placed after them. Structure: Adjective/Adverb + Enough Correct: Smart enough, Old enough, Experienced enough. ✅ Incorrect: Enough smart, Enough old, Enough experienced.❌ 2.With Nouns: When "enough" is used with a noun, it is placed before the noun. Structure: Enough + Noun Correct: Enough money, Enough time, Enough people. ✅

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Quiescent Let’s see the meaning of all words: Misapprehenesion - Misapprehension - गलतफहमी - misunderstanding Example: There was a misapprehension about the meeting time. Brawel - Brawl - लड़ाई - a noisy quarrel or fight Example: The brawl in the bar got out of hand. Tatterd - Tattered - फटे हुए - torn or worn out Example: The tattered book was hard to read. Quiescent - शांत - in a state of quiet or inactivity Example: The volcano has been quiescent for years.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. As soon as the police siren was heard in the distance, the unruly crowd began to __________ from the square.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D) - clear off. 📌 Explanation - The phrasal verb "clear off" (चले जाना/रफूचक्कर होना) means to leave a place quickly, often because you are told to or because you want to avoid trouble. In this context, the crowd is leaving quickly to avoid the police. Clear off - अक्सर किसी मुसीबत से बचने के लिए या आदेश मिलने पर भाग जाना - To leave a place quickly. Example: The boys cleared off as soon as they saw the shopkeeper coming. clear up: साफ करना / स्पष्ट करना - To make something tidy or to solve/explain a mystery/misunderstanding. Example: I hope the weather clears up before the match starts. Clear out: खाली करना / बाहर निकालना - To remove unwanted things from a room, drawer, or cupboard to make it tidy. Example: We need to clear out the attic before we move to the new house. Clear away: हटा देना / जगह साफ करना - To remove objects from a surface (like a table) to leave it empty. Example: Please clear away (बर्तन हटा दो) the breakfast things so I can start working on the table.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – The construction company has been operating in the quarry for years, extracting limestone for building projects.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Game Let’s see the meaning of all words: Quarry - खदान - a place where stone or minerals are extracted Example: The construction company operates in the quarry. Ooze - तरल पदार्थ बहना - to flow or leak slowly Example: Water started to ooze from the broken pipe. Curb - रोकना - to control or limit something Example: The government plans to curb pollution in cities. Game - खेल - an activity or sport, or an animal hunted for sport Example: Football is a popular game worldwide. Dig - खुदाई करना - to break up and move earth or other material Example: The archaeologists began to dig at the ancient site.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The salary offered for the new managerial position is commensurate with the candidate's level of experience and professional qualifications.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D) - No improvement. 📌 Explanation - The sentence is grammatically correct as written. The adjective "commensurate" (अनुरूप/समानुपाती) is a "Fixed Preposition" case and is strictly followed by the preposition "with." Commensurate + With: के अनुरूप / के बराबर / के समानुपाती - Equal in measure or extent; proportionate. Example: Her success was commensurate with her hard work.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Demos
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Nobility Let’s see the meaning of all words; Demos - जनता - the common people or general population Example: The politician connected with the demos during the rally. Fretty - चिड़चिड़ा - annoyed or irritated (not a common word) Example: She was fretty about the delay. Wince - दर्द से झुकना - to show pain or discomfort Example: He winced when he touched the hot stove. Zonal - क्षेत्रीय - relating to a zone or area Example: The zonal manager oversees several states. Nobility - कुलीन वर्ग - people of high social rank or aristocracy Example: The nobility attended the royal ball.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. After several hours of interrogation, the suspect finally admitted __________ the classified documents from the office.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation - The verb "admit" (स्वीकार करना) is followed by a Gerund. We use "stealing" to show the action that the suspect confessed to. to steal: A common error; admit' takes V+ing. stolen: This is a past participle and needs an auxiliary verb (like 'having') to function here. steal: Base form cannot follow another main verb directly without 'to' or becoming a participle.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Get a gold star
options: To earn a merit point for going well
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - To earn a merit point for going well Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Get a gold star - To earn a merit point for doing something well or achieving something notable - पुरस्कार या प्रशंसा मिलना Example: You get a gold star for completing the project on time!

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Each of the students has submitted the assignment on time, __________?
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C) - haven't they. 📌 Explanation - While "Each" is singular and takes a singular verb (has) in the main clause, the rule for Question Tags is different. When the subject of the sentence is Each, Every, Everyone, Someone, or Nobody, the pronoun used in the question tag must be the plural "they." Since the pronoun is plural, the helping verb must also be plural (haven't).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Incisive
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Astute Let’s see the meaning of all words: Incisive - तीक्ष्ण - having a clear and intelligent understanding Example: Her incisive comments on the topic were widely appreciated. Howl - चीखना - to make a loud, long cry Example: The wolf began to howl in the woods. Despot - तानाशाह - a ruler with complete control Example: The despot ruled the country with an iron fist. March - मार्च करना - to walk with a regular step Example: The soldiers began to march towards the border. Astute - बुद्धिमान - having a quick and clever mind Example: She was an astute investor and made smart decisions.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. After years of living in the chaotic and noisy atmosphere of the city, the retired professor decided to spend his remaining years in a _________ setting, surrounded by rolling hills and quiet pastures.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A) - bucolic. 📌 Explanation - The word "Bucolic" (ग्राम्य/देहाती) refers to the pleasant aspects of the countryside and country life. Since the sentence mentions "rolling hills" and "quiet pastures" as a contrast to the "chaotic city," this is the perfect fit. Meanings and explanation of other options: Urban: शहरी - Relating to, or characteristic of a town or city. Example: The urban landscape is dominated by skyscrapers and heavy traffic. Boisterous: शोरगुल वाला / ऊधमी - Noisy, energetic, and cheerful; rowdy. Example: The boisterous crowd cheered loudly when the goal was scored. Bleak: बेरंग / उदास / उजाड़ -Lacking vegetation or exposed to the elements; cold and miserable. Example: The future looked bleak (उदास/अंधकारमय) for the company after the major financial loss. Bucolic: ग्राम्य / देहाती / चरवाही संबंधी - Relating to the pleasant aspects of the countryside and country life. Example: The painting depicted a bucolic scene of shepherds tending to their sheep in a sun-drenched valley.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- Ostracism can be a powerful form of social punishment.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Acceptance Let’s see the meaning of all words: Ostracism - बहिष्कार - exclusion from a group or society Example: The community's ostracism of the family was quite evident. Acceptance - स्वीकृति - approval or agreement Example: The team's acceptance of the new player boosted his confidence. Tangled - उलझा हुआ - twisted together or complicated Example: The wires were tangled and hard to untangle. Probe - जांच करना - to investigate or examine Example: The police will probe the matter further. Aloof - दूर रहना - keeping a distance or being unfriendly Example: He kept himself aloof from the rest of the group.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
options: The diplomatic strategies adopted by the current
administration are far more sophisticated and effective
than the previous government to resolve the crisis.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C). 👉 Replace "than the previous government" with "than those of the previous government". 📌 Error concept: This is an error of Illogical Comparison. The sentence is trying to compare "strategies" (diplomatic strategies) with a "government" (a body of people). You must compare like with like: strategies must be compared with strategies. Grammatical concept: In comparative sentences, we must ensure that the two entities being compared are of the same nature. To avoid repeating the noun "strategies," we use the demonstrative pronoun "those" (for plural) or "that" (for singular). Structure: [Noun A] + Verb + Comparison + than + [that of / those of] + [Noun B].

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. An exclamation of surprise and dismay
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Lordy Let’s see the meaning of all words: Lordy - हे भगवान! - an exclamation of surprise or dismay Example: Lordy, I forgot my phone at home! Pulpit - व्यासपीठ - a raised platform for preaching Example: The priest stood at the pulpit and delivered a powerful sermon. Ovine - भेड़ जैसा - relating to sheep Example: The ovine animals were grazing in the field. Pang - अचानक तेज़ दर्द - a sudden sharp feeling Example: She felt a pang of guilt for forgetting her friend's birthday.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. The scientist was considered an __________ by the country he left behind, but he was welcomed as a highly skilled __________ by the nation where he eventually settled.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B) emigrant, immigrant. 📌 Explanation - This sentence tests the direction of movement. 1.The first blank refers to the country he left (Exit = Emigrant). 2.The second blank refers to the country he entered (In = Immigrant). Therefore, "emigrant" (उत्प्रवासी) and "immigrant" (अप्रवासी) is the correct logical sequence. Emigrant - उत्प्रवासी - A person who leaves their own country to settle permanently in another. Immigrant - अप्रवासी - A person who comes to live permanently in a foreign country.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom. The company hit a bad patch last quarter, but they're recovering.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Experienced difficulty Let’s see the meaning of idiom: Hit a bad patch - एक कठिन दौर का सामना करना (to experience a difficult period)The company hit a bad patch last quarter, but they're recovering.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. P. Crossing the finish line in record-breaking time, she collapsed onto the grass, gasping for air while a triumphant smile spread across her face. Q. The rhythmic thud of her spikes against the synthetic track echoed as she steadily pulled away from the rest of the pack. R. The young athlete stood at the starting block, her heart pounding with a mixture of nervous energy and focused determination. S. As the starter's pistol finally cracked through the morning air, she lunged forward with explosive power and rhythmic grace.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct option is option (C) - RSQP. 📌 Explanation - This sequence follows a Process-Oriented Flow of a 100-meter sprint, moving from the preparation to the finish: R (The Preparation): The scene begins with the athlete at the "starting block." This is the logical starting point of any race (R). S (The Start): The race officially begins with the "pistol crack." This action must follow the athlete being in position (S). Q (The Middle): Once the race starts, we describe the actual running—the "thud of spikes" and the athlete "pulling away" from others (Q). P (The End): The final stage is "crossing the finish line" and the subsequent physical/emotional reaction (P). Final Paragraph: The young athlete stood at the starting block, her heart pounding with a mixture of nervous energy and focused determination. As the starter's pistol finally cracked through the morning air, she lunged forward with explosive power and rhythmic grace. The rhythmic thud of her spikes against the synthetic track echoed as she steadily pulled away from the rest of the pack. Crossing the finish line in record-breaking time, she collapsed onto the grass, gasping for air while a triumphant smile spread across her face.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Devoid of intelligence
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Inane Let’s see the meaning of all words: Palaver - बहस - a lengthy and tedious conversation Example: The meeting turned into a palaver about unnecessary details. Myriad - असंख्य - a large, countless number Example: There are myriad stars in the night sky. Inane - बेवकूफ - lacking intelligence or sense Example: His inane comments made the meeting pointless. Lark - चहचहाना/ मौज - a type of bird or a playful activity Example: We had a lark at the beach yesterday, singing and dancing.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. Why did your brother scold the little boy?
options: Why is the little boy scolded by your brother?
Why has the little boy been scolded by your brother?
Why was the little boy scolded by your brother?
Why was the little boy scolding by your brother?
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C) - Why was the little boy scolded by your brother? 📌 Explanation - The original sentence is in the Active Voice and the Simple Past Tense (did + V1). Rule for Simple Past (Active): Subject + did + V1 + Object? Rule for Simple Past (Passive): Wh-word + was/were + Object + V3 + by + Subject? Since "the little boy" is singular, we use "was" followed by the V3 form of scold, which is "scolded."

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. He said, "If I were you, I would not accept this suspicious offer."
options: He said that if he had been me, he would not accept that suspicious offer.
He said that if he were me, he would not have accepted that suspicious offer.
He advised me that if he were me, he would not accept that suspicious offer.
He said that if he were me, he would not accept that suspicious offer.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D). 📌 Explanation - This is a Type 2 Conditional (If + were + would). Rule: When a sentence expresses a hypothetical situation (If I were you...), the tenses do not shift further back into the past when converted to Indirect speech. Pronoun Change: "I" changes to "he" to match the speaker. Adjective Change: "This" changes to "that" (though "this" is sometimes retained in specific contexts, "that" is the standard SSC transformation). Option C is a common trap; while it sounds like "advice," the structure "He advised me that if he were me" is redundant and grammatically weaker than the direct report of the statement.

questions.question: The persistent challenge of child malnutrition in Adivasi territories remains a complex issue, ____(1)_____ by geographical isolation and the erosion of traditional forest-based diets. Despite various welfare schemes, the delivery of nutritional supplements is often ____(2)_____ by difficult terrain and a lack of culturally sensitive communication. ____(3)______ many families have transitioned from nutrient-dense forest produce to subsidized grains, the resulting "hidden hunger" has led to stunted growth and high infant mortality rates. The government, ____(4)______ the severity of the crisis in recent years, has increased its focus on the Poshan Abhiyaan; however, critics argue that these top-down approaches often fail to empower the community. ____(5)______ bridge this gap, local health workers (ASHA and Anganwadi) must be integrated more deeply into the decision-making process to ensure sustainable health outcomes. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C) - exacerbated. 💢 Explanation: The sentence requires a Past Participle acting as a reduced relative clause (which is exacerbated). It describes how the "complex issue" is made worse by external factors like isolation. Exacerbate - और बिगड़ा हुआ/बदतर - means to make a problem or bad situation worse. Why others are wrong: 1.exacerbating (Pres. Participle): This would mean the "issue" itself is actively making something else worse, which is logically flipped. 2.exacerbate (V1): A base verb cannot follow a comma to modify a preceding noun phrase without a conjunction. 3. having exacerbated: This implies the issue finished making something worse before the main action of the sentence, which doesn't fit the context of a "persistent challenge."

questions.question: The persistent challenge of child malnutrition in Adivasi territories remains a complex issue, ____(1)_____ by geographical isolation and the erosion of traditional forest-based diets. Despite various welfare schemes, the delivery of nutritional supplements is often ____(2)_____ by difficult terrain and a lack of culturally sensitive communication. ____(3)______ many families have transitioned from nutrient-dense forest produce to subsidized grains, the resulting "hidden hunger" has led to stunted growth and high infant mortality rates. The government, ____(4)______ the severity of the crisis in recent years, has increased its focus on the Poshan Abhiyaan; however, critics argue that these top-down approaches often fail to empower the community. ____(5)______ bridge this gap, local health workers (ASHA and Anganwadi) must be integrated more deeply into the decision-making process to ensure sustainable health outcomes. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D) - impeded. 💢 Explanation: The context discusses how "difficult terrain" creates a barrier for the delivery of supplements. Impeded means (बाधित/अवरुद्ध) - to delay or prevent someone or something by obstructing them. Why others are wrong: improved: सुधारा हुआ - This is the opposite of the required meaning; difficult terrain does not help delivery. bolstered: मजबूत किया - This means to support or strengthen, which contradicts the "difficult terrain" context. facilitating: सुगम बनाना - This means making a process easy. Grammatically, "is often facilitating by" is incorrect passive voice construction; it would need to be "facilitated by."

questions.question: The persistent challenge of child malnutrition in Adivasi territories remains a complex issue, ____(1)_____ by geographical isolation and the erosion of traditional forest-based diets. Despite various welfare schemes, the delivery of nutritional supplements is often ____(2)_____ by difficult terrain and a lack of culturally sensitive communication. ____(3)______ many families have transitioned from nutrient-dense forest produce to subsidized grains, the resulting "hidden hunger" has led to stunted growth and high infant mortality rates. The government, ____(4)______ the severity of the crisis in recent years, has increased its focus on the Poshan Abhiyaan; however, critics argue that these top-down approaches often fail to empower the community. ____(5)______ bridge this gap, local health workers (ASHA and Anganwadi) must be integrated more deeply into the decision-making process to ensure sustainable health outcomes. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D) - Since. 💢 Explanation: In this context, "Since" is used as a conjunction meaning "because" or "given that." it explains the reason why the "hidden hunger" occurred (the transition from forest produce to grains). Why others are wrong: Moreover: This is an adverb used to add information, but it cannot connect two clauses within the same sentence like a subordinating conjunction. Because: While similar to "Since," "Since" is often preferred when the reason is already known to the reader or established in the narrative. However, "Since" also fits the temporal shift ("Since many families have transitioned..."). Although: This indicates contrast, but the sentence describes a cause-and-effect relationship, not a contradiction.

questions.question: The persistent challenge of child malnutrition in Adivasi territories remains a complex issue, ____(1)_____ by geographical isolation and the erosion of traditional forest-based diets. Despite various welfare schemes, the delivery of nutritional supplements is often ____(2)_____ by difficult terrain and a lack of culturally sensitive communication. ____(3)______ many families have transitioned from nutrient-dense forest produce to subsidized grains, the resulting "hidden hunger" has led to stunted growth and high infant mortality rates. The government, ____(4)______ the severity of the crisis in recent years, has increased its focus on the Poshan Abhiyaan; however, critics argue that these top-down approaches often fail to empower the community. ____(5)______ bridge this gap, local health workers (ASHA and Anganwadi) must be integrated more deeply into the decision-making process to ensure sustainable health outcomes. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B) - recognizing. 💢 Explanation: We need a Present Participle phrase to describe the government's current/ongoing state of awareness. "The government, recognizing the severity..." acts like a non-finite clause explaining why they increased focus. Recognize - पहचाना/मान्यता प्राप्त Why others are wrong: recognized: As a past participle here, it would imply the government is being recognized by someone else, rather than the government doing the recognizing. recognizes: This is a finite verb. You cannot place a finite verb between two commas as a modifier for the subject without a relative pronoun (e.g., "which recognizes"). to recognize: This indicates future purpose, but the context implies they have already seen the crisis and are currently responding.

questions.question: The persistent challenge of child malnutrition in Adivasi territories remains a complex issue, ____(1)_____ by geographical isolation and the erosion of traditional forest-based diets. Despite various welfare schemes, the delivery of nutritional supplements is often ____(2)_____ by difficult terrain and a lack of culturally sensitive communication. ____(3)______ many families have transitioned from nutrient-dense forest produce to subsidized grains, the resulting "hidden hunger" has led to stunted growth and high infant mortality rates. The government, ____(4)______ the severity of the crisis in recent years, has increased its focus on the Poshan Abhiyaan; however, critics argue that these top-down approaches often fail to empower the community. ____(5)______ bridge this gap, local health workers (ASHA and Anganwadi) must be integrated more deeply into the decision-making process to ensure sustainable health outcomes. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D) - In order to. 💢 Explanation: This phrase is used to express purpose. The purpose of integrating local health workers is "to bridge this gap." It is followed by the base form of the verb ("bridge"). In order to - के उद्देश्य से/ताकि Why others are wrong: So that: ताकि - This must be followed by a full clause (subject + verb), e.g., "So that they can bridge..." Consequently: परिणामस्वरूप - This is a transition word showing a result, usually starting a new sentence, not introducing an infinitive of purpose. Notwithstanding: के बावजूद - This means "in spite of" or "despite," which does not fit the goal-oriented logic of the sentence.

questions.question: In this question given below is a statement followed by two conclusions. You have to assume everything in the statement to be true and on the basis of the information given in the statement, decide which of the following conclusions can be logically deduced from the statement. Statement: A city report reads that the number of homeless people has increased over the past five years due to rising housing costs and unemployment. The municipal corporation has proposed constructing low-cost rental housing units and creating job-oriented skill programs. Conclusions : I. Providing affordable houses and employment opportunities may reduce homelessness in the city. II. Rising housing costs and unemployment were not prevalent before the five-year period mentioned in the report.
options: Neither Conclusion I nor Conclusion II follows.
Both Conclusion I and Conclusion II follows.
questions.explanation: Conclusion 1 follows as problem is linked to housing costs and unemployment and this conclusion directly addresses these problems. Conclusion 2 doesn't follow as the statement never says these are the only reasons and use of "only" makes the conclusion extreme. only I follows Opt 1

questions.question: Hedonist is related to Pleasure in the same way as Petrified is related to?
questions.explanation: Hedonist and Pleasure are synonyms Petrified and Frightened are synonyms. opt 3

questions.question: Three of the following letter-number pairs are alike in some manner and hence form a group. Select the one which is not part of the group?

questions.question: Three statements are given, followed by two conclusions numbered I and II. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All erasers are pencils. Some pencils are sharpener. No sharpener is a scale. Conclusions: I. Some erasers are sharpener. II. No eraser is a scale.

Gwen sits between Dave and Avi. Rosy does not sit at one of the extreme ends. Who sits second to the right of Gwen?

questions.question: Post: A company is hiring for the post of Customer Service Executive. Eligibility Criteria: 1. The candidate must be between 21 and 28 years old (both inclusive). 2. He has 2 years of work experience in telecom industry. Question: What decision should be made in Nishant’s case?
options: Refer the case to the HR team

questions.question: ‘POLLUTION’ is related to NOITULLOP in the same way as ‘ANSWER’ is related to?
questions.explanation: POLLUTION → Reverse the order from right to left:- NOITULLOP ANSWER → Reverse the order from, right to left.' REWSNA opt4

questions.question: Select the option that represents the letters that, when placed from left to right in the following blanks, will complete the letter-series? BM_F_B_XF_BM_ _Q
questions.explanation: BMXFQ|BMXFQ|BMXFQ opt 3

questions.question: Six friends: K, L, M, N ,O,P are sitting around a circular table facing the center but not necessarily in the same order. K sits second to the left of M. L sits immediate right of K. N sits opposite to K. O is an immediate neighbor of P. L and O are not sitting opposite to each other. In the final arrangement, if M and K interchange their positions, how many persons are sitting between M and N when counted from the left of M ?
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774247163Rsng%20Paid%20-%2012_html_5b51011b.png) When K and M interchange: - ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774247163Rsng%20Paid%20-%2012_html_5ed1a782.png) Two people O ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774247163Rsng%20Paid%20-%2012_html_ff5225f3.gif) sitting b/w M & N when counted from the left of M opt 2

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third letter cluster in the same way as the second letter cluster is related to the first letter cluster:- BRACKET: ABCEKRT :: HEALTHY : ?
questions.explanation: Alphabetical order arrangement :- BRACKET → ABCEKRT HEALTHY → AEHHLTY opt 1

questions.question: You are a member of the Scholarship Selection Committee in a college. During final evaluation, you realize that one of the shortlisted candidates is your close cousin. He meets the minimum eligibility criteria but is not the strongest candidate compared to others. No one else in the committee is aware of your relationship with him. What will be the most appropriate action?
options: Support your cousin since he meets the eligibility criteria and family comes first.
Remain silent about the relationship and let the committee decide without your disclosure.
Inform the committee about your relationship and excuse yourself from the evaluation process.
Reject your cousin immediately to avoid any suspicion.
questions.explanation: By informing the committee and excusing yourself you will:- (1) Maintain integrity. (2) Avoid bias (3) Protect credibility of the selection process. opt 3

questions.question: In a certain year, 15th August falls on Wednesday. If the year is a non-leap year, what day of the week will be 1st January of the next year ?
questions.explanation: 15 ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774247163Rsng%20Paid%20-%2012_html_781a4a0.gif) Aug → Wednesday Extra Days:- Aug → 2 Sept → 2 Oct → 3 Nov → 2 Dec → 3 Jan → 1 Total extra = 13/7 → (6) 1st Jan → Wed + 6 = Tuesday opt 3

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term :- Lira : Turkey :: Franc : ?
questions.explanation: Lira is the currency of Turkey Franc is the currency of Switzerland opt4
